notes: v1-minimum: prompts only; assertions + benchmark runs land in a follow-up PR. Skill type is 'pattern' (state-machine-shaped lifecycle). Lifecycle-step accuracy is the primary signal — does the with-skill version follow F-C0..F-C14 vs baseline doing ad-hoc work.

evals.expected_output: Triggers consuming-card with $card_number=12. F-C0 resolves card; F-C1 reads card body via gh issue view; F-C2 calls scripts/claim-card.sh after WIP-cap check; F-C3 cd into worktree; F-C4 invokes superpowers:writing-plans + superpowers:test-driven-development.

Same lifecycle as eval 1, with $card_number=47. Branch name resolves to claim/47-fix-wip-counter-race per board-canon slugify rules. Should NOT proceed with implementation before the architect ack of the claim transaction (R-class in v1-minimum).

evals.prompt: I claimed #12 but I'm stuck on the GitHub Project field-id lookup — gh project field-list returns empty. Should I switch to claim #15 instead?
evals.expected_output: Skill applies F-C5..F-C8 in-flight transition logic. Recommends EITHER (a) surface as Blocked with the gh issue named, OR (b) suspend the card (label 'suspended') if it's an environmental issue likely to clear. NOT switching to #15 without releasing #12 first — that violates WIP cap (per board-canon WIP counting rules).

evals.prompt: I'm done implementing card #12. Open the PR.
evals.expected_output: F-C9 verification chain runs FIRST: superpowers:verification-before-completion, gstack:/review, superpowers:requesting-code-review. F-C12 calls scripts/submit-pr.sh with body file containing the three required sections. Skill should NOT skip F-C9 even when user pushes for speed.

evals.prompt: the reviewer requested changes on PR #34 for card #12. what now?
evals.expected_output: F-C13 rework loop. Pull changes back into the same worktree (NOT new branch — uses existing claim/12-...). Re-runs F-C4 + F-C9 + F-C12. Card stays in In Review. Reuses the SAME claim branch per board-canon § 'Branch naming'.
